title: abutilon benedictum
intro: Abutilon benedictum, also known as Indian mallow or musk mallow, is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. Abutilon benedictum is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other medicinal products.
